docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
This user guide will demonstrate the basics of working with GitHub and Git. In this documentation we will go over creating a new repository in GitHub, pushing an existing project into it and working with an exiting repo (someone else's code) in GitHub.

Note

:material-github: GitHub is a website that lets you host git repositories. It is used for collaboration with other developers, storing and reviewing code.

:material-git: Git is a Version Control System used to track files and changes in your code over time.

docs.title: Intended Users
docs.text: This documentation is intended for individuals who have no knowledge of Github or Version Control Systems.
